title: How to set up Two-Factor Authentication (2FA)
description: Learn how to add an extra layer of security to your online accounts to block hackers completely.

steps.title: Log in and open Security Settings
steps.body: Go to the website you want to secure (like Google, Facebook, or your bank). Log in and find the 'Settings' or 'Security' panel.

steps.title: Find Two-Factor Authentication
steps.body: Look for a setting named 'Two-Factor Authentication', '2FA', or 'Two-Step Verification' and click turn on.

steps.title: Choose your verification method
steps.body: Choose 'Text Message' (SMS) if you want a short code texted to your phone, or 'Authenticator App' if you want to use an app like Google Authenticator.

steps.title: Enter the backup code to verify
steps.body: Type the verification code shown on your phone into the website screen to link your device. Write down any recovery codes provided and save them safely.

glossary.definition: A security setup where you must provide two pieces of evidence to log in: your password and a temporary code sent to your phone.
A secure application on your phone that generates fresh login verification codes every 30 seconds.
